001package react4j.annotations;
002
003import arez.annotations.ActAsComponent;
004import arez.annotations.ArezComponent;
005import arez.annotations.Memoize;
006import arez.annotations.Observable;
007import arez.annotations.Observe;
008import arez.component.Identifiable;
009import java.lang.annotation.Documented;
010import java.lang.annotation.ElementType;
011import java.lang.annotation.Target;
012import javax.annotation.Nonnull;
013import react4j.Keyed;
014
015/**
016 * Annotation used to specify an input.
017 * The property is extracted from Reacts underlying props object. By default the input is passed as a
018 * value in when creating the view but it can also be retrieved from the react context.
019 * When applied to an abstract method, the input can be mutable or immutable.
020 * When applied to a constructor parameter, the input must set {@link #immutable()} to {@code true}.
021 *
022 * <p>If applied to a method, the method that is annotated with this annotation must also comply with the
023 * following constraints:</p>
024 * <ul>
025 * <li>Must not be annotated with any other react annotation</li>
026 * <li>Must have 0 parameters</li>
027 * <li>Must return a value</li>
028 * <li>Must be an abstract instance method</li>
029 * <li>Must not throw exceptions</li>
030 * <li>Must be accessible from the same package as the class annotated by {@link View}</li>
031 * <li>
032 *   Should not be public as not expected to be invoked outside the view. A warning will be generated but can
033 *   be suppressed by the {@link SuppressWarnings} or {@link SuppressReact4jWarnings} annotations with a key
034 *   "React4j:PublicMethod". This warning is also suppressed by the annotation processor if it is implementing
035 *   an interface method.
036 * </li>
037 * <li>
038 *   Should not be protected if in the class annotated with the {@link View} annotation as the method is not
039 *   expected to be invoked outside the view. A warning will be generated but can be suppressed by the
040 *   {@link SuppressWarnings} or {@link SuppressReact4jWarnings} annotations with a key "React4j:ProtectedMethod".
041 * </li>
042 * <li>
043 *   Should be annotated with either {@link javax.annotation.Nonnull} or {@link javax.annotation.Nullable} if the
044 *   return type is not a primitive. A warning will be generated but can be suppressed by the {@link SuppressWarnings}
045 *   or {@link SuppressReact4jWarnings} annotations with a key "React4j:MissingInputNullability".
046 * </li>
047 * </ul>
048 */
049@Documented
050@Target( { ElementType.METHOD, ElementType.PARAMETER } )
051public @interface Input
052{
053  /**
054   * Return the name of the input.
055   * The name is the key used when accessing the input from the inputs object. It is also used when creating
056   * the builder steps associated with the inputs that set {@link #source()} to {@link Source#DEFAULT}.
057   *
058   * @return the name of the input.
059   */
060  @Nonnull
061  String name() default "<default>";
062
063  /**
064   * Return the qualifier used to access value from context.
065   * It must only be specified if {@link #source()} is set to {@link Source#CONTEXT}.
066   *
067   * @return the qualifier used to access value from context.
068   */
069  @Nonnull
070  String qualifier() default "";
071
072  /**
073   * The setting controlling where the input value is source from.
074   * If the source is set to {@link Source#CONTEXT} then the input is sometimes described as a "TreeInput"
075   * as it is transparently passed from a parent view to all child views. A "TreeInput" does not
076   * have to be specified by the user when creating the view.
077   *
078   * @return the setting controlling where the input value is source from.
079   */
080  Source source() default Source.DEFAULT;
081
082  /**
083   * Setting indicating whether the input should be supplied when the view is constructed.
084   * This influences validation when enabled and how the Builder class is created.
085   * If set to {@link Feature#ENABLE} then the user MUST supply the input and the builder will require the user
086   * to specify the value. If set to {@link Feature#DISABLE} then the user can optionally supply the input.
087   * If set to {@link Feature#AUTODETECT} then the annotation processor will treat it as {@link Feature#DISABLE}
088   * if there is a corresponding {@link InputDefault} for the input or the {@link #source()} parameter is set to
089   * {@link Source#CONTEXT}, otherwise it will be treated as {@link Feature#ENABLE}. The value of this setting
090   * must not be {@link Feature#ENABLE} when {@link #source()} is set to {@link Source#CONTEXT}.
091   *
092   * @return the flag indicating whether the input needs to be supplied.
093   */
094  Feature require() default Feature.AUTODETECT;
095
096  /**
097   * Indicate whether the input should be annotated by {@link Observable}.
098   *
099   * <p>If set to {@link Feature#AUTODETECT} then the input will be observable if and only if:</p>
100   * <ul>
101   * <li>{@link #immutable()} ()} is not set to {@code true}.</li>
102   * <li>the view has at least one method annotated with {@link Memoize} or {@link Observe}.</li>
103   * </ul>
104   *
105   * @return the enum indicating whether input is observable.
106   */
107  Feature observable() default Feature.AUTODETECT;
108
109  /**
110   * Return an enum indicating whether the view should check whether the value of the input is disposed
111   * prior to rendering. If the value is disposed then the render method will exit early and return null.
112   * If this parameter is set to {@link Feature#AUTODETECT} then the annotation processor will inspect the
113   * type of the input and treat it as {@link Feature#ENABLE} if the type is annotated with the {@link ArezComponent}
114   * annotation or the {@link ActAsComponent} annotation.
115   *
116   * @return an enum indicating whether the view should check whether the value of the input is disposed prior to rendering.
117   */
118  Feature disposable() default Feature.AUTODETECT;
119
120  /**
121   * Return an enum indicating whether tracked renders should observe the input value during the generated render
122   * prelude so that disposing the input can invalidate the render and trigger a rerender.
123   * This parameter is independent of {@link #disposable()} and is only relevant to views with render tracking enabled.
124   *
125   * <p>If this parameter is set to {@link Feature#AUTODETECT} then the annotation processor will inspect the
126   * type of the input and treat it as {@link Feature#ENABLE} in the following circumstances:</p>
127   * <ul>
128   * <li>the type is compatible with {@code arez.component.ComponentObservable}.</li>
129   * <li>the type is annotated with {@link ArezComponent} and the {@link ArezComponent#observable()} parameter
130   * resolves to enabled.</li>
131   * <li>the type is annotated with {@link ActAsComponent}. In this scenario the generated code will verify
132   * compatibility with {@code arez.component.ComponentObservable} at runtime before observing the value.</li>
133   * </ul>
134   *
135   * @return an enum indicating whether tracked renders should observe the input value during render.
136   */
137  Feature observeOnRender() default Feature.AUTODETECT;
138
139  /**
140   * Return an enum indicating whether the view should be disposed if the input is disposed. To enable this feature,
141   * the input MUST set {@link #immutable()} to <code>true</code>, {@link #disposable()} MUST resolve to
142   * {@link Feature#ENABLE}. The type of the input is expected to implement the {@link arez.component.DisposeNotifier}
143   * interface either directly or indirectly. If this parameter is set to {@link Feature#AUTODETECT} then the
144   * annotation processor will treat it as {@link Feature#ENABLE} if {@link #immutable()} is <code>true</code> and
145   * {@link #disposable()} resolves to {@link Feature#ENABLE}.
146   *
147   * @return an enum indicating whether the view should be disposed if the input is disposed.
148   */
149  Feature dependency() default Feature.AUTODETECT;
150
151  /**
152   * True if the input is not expected to change after initial value is set. If the value of the input does change
153   * then it is expected that the view will be unmounted and a new view created. This is implemented
154   * by synthesizing a key for the view every time the view that is derived from this input. To enable this
155   * the annotation processor must be able to identify the type of the input so that a key can be synthesized. The
156   * following types are supported by the annotation processor;
157   *
158   * <ul>
159   * <li>primitive types (i.e. boolean, short etc) and their corresponding boxed types (i.e. {@link java.lang.Boolean}, {@link java.lang.Short} etc).</li>
160   * <li>the {@link java.lang.String} type</li>
161   * <li>any class that implements {@link Keyed}</li>
162   * <li>any class that is annotated with {@link ArezComponent} where the {@link ArezComponent#requireId()} parameter does not resolve to {@link arez.annotations.Feature#DISABLE}</li>
163   * <li>any class or interface that is annotated with {@link ActAsComponent}. It is assumed that every implementation is an Arez component where the {@link ArezComponent#requireId()} parameter does not resolve to {@link arez.annotations.Feature#DISABLE}</li>
164   * <li>any class or interface that is compatible with {@link Identifiable}</li>
165   * <li>if none of the above scenarios is valid then the code will attempt to derive the key at runtime. First via the {@link Keyed} interface, then the {@link Identifiable} interface and if these strategies are not possible then toString() will be invoked on the input.</li>
166   * </ul>
167   *
168   * <p>It should be noted that if a type implements {@link Keyed} and is annotated with either {@link ArezComponent}
169   * or {@link ActAsComponent} then the annotation processor will assume the {@link Keyed} interface is to used in
170   * preference to other alternative strategies.</p>
171   *
172   * @return true if changing the input recreates the view.
173   */
174  boolean immutable() default false;
175
176  /**
177   * Enum where the input is sourced from.
178   */
179  enum Source
180  {
181    /**
182     * The input value is passed to the view during construction.
183     */
184    DEFAULT,
185    /**
186     * The input value is retrieved from the react context.
187     */
188    CONTEXT
189  }
190}
